questions.question: A basketball team scored $48$ points. Is $48$ divisible by $3$?
The distance between two tennis courts is $150$ meters. Is $150$ divisible by $5$?
A cyclist traveled $63$ kilometers. Is $63$ divisible by $9$?
A marathon runner completed $120$ miles. Is $120$ divisible by $10$?
A gymnast performed $18$ routines. Is $18$ divisible by $2$?
The score in a football game is $75$ to $42$. Is the difference ($75 - 42$) divisible by $3$?
A athlete won $99$ medals. Is $99$ divisible by $9$?
The number of spectators at a sports event is $240$. Is $240$ divisible by $10$?

A long-distance runner ran $237$ kilometers. Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when $237$ is divided by $12$. Express the result as $12q + r$, where $q$ is the quotient and $r$ is the remainder.

In a $400$-meter dash, a sprinter ran $312$ meters in the first part of the race. If the remaining distance is divided into segments of $8$ meters each, how many segments will the sprinter have to run to complete the race? Show your work using the division algorithm ($div$ and $mod$ operations).

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to check their work using multiplication
Tip 2: Remind students to apply the correct divisibility rules for each divisor
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of remainder in real-life applications
